title: Opiate Toxidrome
body_html:
A toxidrome refers to a set of symptoms and signs which occur following overdose of a particular class of drug. Commonly seen toxidromes are cholinergic, anticholingeric, sympathomimetic, opiate and sedative.

Opiate compounds, e.g. heroin, morphine and fentanyl, stimulate opiate receptors.


Key features of the opiate toxidrome:
  • Respiratory depression
  • Pin-point pupils
  • Hypotension
  • Drowsiness
